title: LinkedIn Profile Optimization
description: Transform your LinkedIn profile into a magnet for recruiters, collaborators, and career opportunities.

modules.title: Profile Essentials
modules.description: Set up the core sections recruiters look at first.

lessons.title: Profile Photo and Banner Best Practices
Writing a Headline That Opens Doors
lessons.content:
# Writing a LinkedIn Headline That Opens Doors

Your headline is the first thing people see. The default is your job title — do better.

## The Formula

`[What you do] | [Who you help] | [Key outcome or value]`

## Examples

**Default (weak):** Marketing Manager at Acme Corp

**Optimised:** B2B Content Strategist | Helping SaaS companies grow organic traffic | SEO & Thought Leadership

## Tips
- Use keywords recruiters search for.
- Include your industry niche.
- Avoid buzzwords: passionate, results-driven, guru.
- You have 220 characters — use them.

lessons.title: Crafting Your About Section
lessons.content:
# Crafting Your About Section

The About section is your 2,600-character story. Most people leave it blank or paste their resume.

## Structure

1. **Hook** (first 2 lines, visible before

) — grab attention with your core value or a compelling statement.
2. **Your story** — why you do what you do.
3. **What you bring** — 3–5 key skills or achievements.
4. **Call to action** — invite connections, collaborations, or conversations.

## Writing Tips
- Write in first person.
- Use short paragraphs and line breaks for readability.
- Include keywords naturally.
- End with your email or best contact method.

modules.title: Experience, Skills and Recommendations
modules.description: Build the sections that prove your credibility.

lessons.title: Adding Quantified Experience Entries
lessons.content:
# Adding Quantified Experience Entries

Treat each role like a resume bullet — show impact, not just duties.

## Format
- Start with a one-sentence role summary.
- Follow with 3–5 bullet points using the CAR method (Context, Action, Result).
- Include keywords relevant to your target roles.

## Example

**Customer Success Manager — TechCorp (2021–Present)**
Manage a portfolio of 35 mid-market SaaS accounts worth $2.4 M ARR.
- Reduced churn by 22 % through a proactive health-score monitoring programme.
- Grew net revenue retention to 118 % by identifying upsell opportunities during QBRs.
- Onboarded 12 new enterprise accounts in Q1 2024, achieving 100 % adoption within 60 days.
